analysis.overview: PM-SETU (Pradhan Mantri Skills Enhancement and Training for Upskilling) is a centrally sponsored scheme under the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) aimed at enhancing the employability of youth through industry-aligned skill training programs. It focuses on bridging the skill gap by providing training in emerging and high-demand sectors, with an emphasis on placement-linked outcomes. The scheme operates through a network of Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendras (PMKKs), and other accredited training partners across India.
objectives: Provide industry-relevant skill training to enhance employability of youth
Align training programs with National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF)
Ensure placement linkage for at least 70% of certified trainees
Promote inclusion of disadvantaged and marginalized groups in skill development
Strengthen public-private partnership in vocational training delivery
Upgrade infrastructure and training capacity of ITIs and PMKKs
Monitor and evaluate training outcomes through a robust MIS system
analysis.eligibility: Indian nationals aged between 15 and 45 years, with minimum educational qualification as per the specific job role (ranging from 5th pass to 12th pass or equivalent), belonging to any category including SC/ST/OBC/Minorities/PwD, and meeting the eligibility criteria defined for the specific trade or course under the scheme.
analysis.benefits: Free skill training in NSQF-aligned courses, stipend during training (where applicable), placement assistance, post-placement tracking, certification recognized by NCVT or SSC, industry exposure through on-the-job training, and support for self-employment or wage employment.
analysis.financial_support: The scheme provides 100% funding for training costs, including trainer remuneration, raw materials, assessment, and certification fees. In certain cases, a stipend is provided to trainees during the training period, especially for women, SC/ST, and persons with disabilities, as per the norms of the specific project or implementing agency.
analysis.application_process: 1. Identify the desired skill course and nearest training center (ITI/PMKK/accredited partner). 2. Visit the center or apply online through the State Skill Development Mission portal. 3. Fill out the application form with personal and educational details. 4. Submit required documents for verification. 5. Undergo counseling and pre-assessment if required. 6. Get enrolled in the selected course. 7. Attend training as per schedule. 8. Appear for final assessment. 9. Receive certification and placement support.
required_documents: Educational Certificates (Mark sheets and passing certificates)
Bank account details (for stipend, if applicable)
analysis.deadlines: Rolling basis — admissions are open throughout the year based on batch commencement schedules at training centers.
analysis.implementing_agency: Directorate General of Training (DGT), Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE)
caveats: Placement is not guaranteed but facilitated through industry linkages
Stipend is not provided in all courses and depends on the project and trainee category
Training duration and curriculum vary by job role and sector
Certification is awarded only upon successful completion of assessment
Beneficiaries must maintain minimum attendance as per norms to continue in the program
